title: GameStop (GME) Q4 2022 Earnings Results Beat Expectations
text: GameStop exceeded Wall Street and Whisper Number expectations in its Q4 2022 earnings report, delivering positive results across key financial metrics despite widespread predictions of losses. GameStop reported gains of $0.16 per share, contrasting sharply with Wall Streets expected loss of $0.13 and Whisper Numbers projected loss of $0.20. The company achieved net sales of $2.23 billion, surpassing Wall Streets average estimate of $2.2 billion. Hardware sales led the charge, representing $1.24 billion or approximately 56% of total net sales. Collectibles also showed strength, reaching $313 million compared to $279 million in the prior years quarter. Software sales declined to $670 million from the previous year. GameStop generated positive cash flow for the second consecutive quarter, reporting $177.3 million in Q4 2022, following $338.2 million in Q3 2022. The strong earnings results drove GME stock higher in after-hours trading on March 21, 2023.
